workflow.name: SDLC Assessment Request Changes
workflow.goal: Ship a CLI enhancement where revision 1 adds the core assess command and tests, the acceptance assessor requests changes for missing release-readiness fields and docs, and revision 2 resolves those findings before release packaging proceeds.

parameters.initial_revision_scope: Revision 1 MUST add `python3 -m workflow_cli assess --scenario-name <name>` that emits deterministic JSON with scenario_name, assessment_status=draft, and release_ready=false, plus unit coverage for the new command. Revision 1 MUST NOT add package_reference output or README usage for the assess command yet.
parameters.rework_completion_scope: After request_changes, revision 2 MUST update the assess command to emit deterministic JSON with scenario_name, assessment_contract echo, assessment_status=approved, release_ready=true, and a non-empty package_reference. Revision 2 MUST also update README usage and unit coverage so the repository fully documents and verifies the assess command.
parameters.assessment_contract: The acceptance assessor must request changes once when the linked subject only satisfies the initial revision scope, citing the missing release-ready fields and README coverage. The assessor must approve only after one real rework cycle increases the subject revision and the linked subject satisfies the rework completion scope.

instructions.content: This scenario uses an intentionally staged two-revision delivery contract. Revision 1 MUST stop at `initial_revision_scope`. Do not satisfy any requirement that appears only in `rework_completion_scope` until the implementation task is explicitly reopened after `request_changes`. Treat early delivery of `rework_completion_scope` items as contract noncompliance, not as proactive helpfulness.
